name: Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop
name_original: Giro ad anello da Folgosinho verso Cabeço da Azinha — Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela
description: Tackle the challenging Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop, a 27.9-mile mountain bike ride with 4270 feet of climbing in Serra da Estrela.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Folgosinho offers diverse terrain within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, Portugal's largest protected area. The region is characterized by dramatic mountainous landscapes, including glacial valleys like the Zêzere River's upper valley, high plateaus, and granite formations. Riders can expect a mix of forest roads and single trails, with varied surfaces including natural and gravel roads, providing a range of experiences. The area features significant elevation changes, contributing to challenging routes. Best mountain bike trails in Folgosinho The most popular mountain bike trail is From Manteigas towards Lake Comprida – Serra da Estrela Natural Park, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views across the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Through the glacial valley of Zêzere – Serra da Estrela Natural Park, a difficult 29.4 miles (47.3 km) path. This route navigates a distinct glacial valley with challenging descents and unique geological formations. Local mountain bikers also love the Loop from Manteigas to Covão da Ponte – Serra da Estrela Natural Park, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 2 minutes. Mountain biking around Folgosinho is defined by glacial valleys, high plateaus, and granite formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present a significant challenge. The routes in Folgosinho are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Folgosinho's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Folgosinho?
A: The mountain bike trails around Folgosinho, located within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, are generally challenging. While there are some moderate routes, the majority of the 10 available trails are classified as difficult. Riders can expect significant elevation changes and varied terrain, including technically demanding descents and granite formations. For example, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330365737/from-manteigas-towards-lake-comprida-serra-da-estrela-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Manteigas towards Lake Comprida&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330285159/through-the-glacial-valley-of-zezere-serra-da-estrela-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Through the glacial valley of Zêzere&lt;/a&gt; are rated as difficult, requiring good fitness and technical skills.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Folgosinho?
A: Mountain biking around Folgosinho is primarily geared towards more experienced riders, with most routes classified as moderate to difficult. There are no trails specifically designated as 'easy' in the immediate area. The terrain often features significant elevation changes and varied surfaces, which might be challenging for beginners or young children. If you are looking for less demanding options, consider exploring the region's forest roads, which might offer a gentler experience, though they may not be dedicated mountain bike trails.

Q: Can I find waterfalls along mountain bike routes in Folgosinho?
A: Yes, the region around Folgosinho is home to beautiful natural features, including waterfalls. The spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço do Inferno (Hell's Pit) waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, carved by water through granite, is a notable attraction. While not directly on every mountain bike route, it is a significant landmark in the area and can be accessed via trails. You might also encounter other cascades, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4416197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Road to Hell's Pit&lt;/a&gt;, adding to the scenic beauty of your ride.

Q: What kind of viewpoints or landmarks can I expect to see while mountain biking in Folgosinho?
A: Folgosinho offers magnificent panoramic views and historical landmarks. From spots like Cabeço da Azinha, you can enjoy uninterrupted vistas over the dynamic interplay of reliefs and depressions of the Serra da Estrela. The medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/2554261/attractions-around-folgosinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Folgosinho&lt;/a&gt;, though rebuilt, provides breathtaking views over the horizon. Other notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7366287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Mondego Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3477370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful lake in the high plateaus.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Folgosinho suitable for riding with dogs?
A: The trails around Folgosinho are located within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, which is Portugal's largest protected area. While many natural parks allow dogs, it's important to be aware of and respect local regulations regarding pets, especially concerning wildlife protection and leash requirements. The region is rich in biodiversity, home to mammals like wolves, boars, and foxes, so keeping dogs under control is crucial. Always check specific park guidelines before heading out with your dog.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Folgosinho?
A: The Serra da Estrela region, including Folgosinho, experiences distinct seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at lower altitudes, while winter brings colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations, which can make some trails impassable or more challenging. Always check local weather forecasts before planning your ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Folgosinho?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes in the wider Folgosinho area. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330306349/loop-from-manteigas-to-covao-da-ponte-serra-da-estrela-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Manteigas to Covão da Ponte&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult route that takes you through mountainous terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330590394/loop-from-manteigas-towards-sameiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Manteigas towards Sameiro&lt;/a&gt;, also a difficult circular trail. These loops allow riders to start and finish in the same general area, offering a convenient way to explore the diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Folgosinho?
A: The mountain bike routes in Folgosinho are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. Riders frequently praise the dramatic mountainous landscapes, the unique glacial valleys, and the challenging nature of the trails. The panoramic views from high plateaus and the diverse ecosystems, including shady trees and rich flora, also contribute to a highly enjoyable and immersive riding experience.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Folgosinho?
A: Folgosinho is a traditional village with granite houses and cobbled streets, offering a charming base for your mountain biking adventures. You can find local cafes and accommodation options within the village itself. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2574334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim River Beach Restaurant&lt;/a&gt; is located near the Vale do Rossim Reservoir, which is accessible from some trails. Exploring the village will provide opportunities to experience local culture and cuisine after your ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Folgosinho?
A: The terrain around Folgosinho is highly varied, reflecting the diverse landscape of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. You will encounter a mix of forest roads and singletracks. Surfaces range from paved sections to natural dirt paths and gravel roads. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, glacial valleys, high plateaus, and granite formations, providing a dynamic and often challenging riding experience.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
A: While the Serra da Estrela Natural Park is a protected area, general mountain biking on established trails typically does not require specific individual permits for access. However, it is always advisable to respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of the natural environment and wildlife. For any specific events or organized group activities, it's best to check with the park authorities for potential requirements.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in the Folgosinho area?
A: There are 10 mountain bike trails available in the Folgosinho area, offering a range of experiences for riders. These routes vary in length and difficulty, with 3 classified as moderate and 7 as difficult, ensuring options for those seeking a challenge within the stunning landscapes of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park.
tips:
The village is famous for its granite houses, cobbled streets and its imposing medieval castle, which offers spectacular views of the surrounding landscape. 

Legend has it that Folgosinho was founded by Lusone, grandson of Noah, which adds a touch of mysticism to its already fascinating history.

This elevated location is particularly appreciated for its uninterrupted view, which extends over an accumulation of reliefs and depressions, creating a dynamic and engaging landscape. 

In addition to being a breathtaking observation point, Cabeço da Azinha is also an important site for air sports enthusiasts, as it hosts a paragliding runway that hosts national and international events. Its elevated position also makes it a strategic point for the fire observation post, Ponto de Vigia Gorgulão. The local flora is rich and varied, with species such as the kermes oak (azinha, from which it takes its name), heather and rosemary, which contribute to the wild beauty of the place.
Here you ride on a beautiful gravel road with a wide and flat surface. On one side you have fields and on the other a thick forest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking loop is quite challenging and covers a distance of nearly 45 kilometers with over 1300 meters of elevation gain. On average, it takes about 4 hours and 25 minutes to complete, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level and riding speed.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and challenging, characteristic of the wild and rural landscape around Folgosinho. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including gravel roads, cobbled streets in villages like Folgosinho, and potentially technical singletrack sections with features like rock gardens. Expect significant hills and deep valleys, and be prepared for sections where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: Is the Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is classified as 'difficult' and is not recommended for beginners. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to significant elevation gains and technical sections. Riders should be comfortable with challenging descents and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts and ends in the village of Folgosinho. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a historical village, there are usually designated parking spots available within or on the outskirts of the settlement. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this loop are generally during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cold temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact loop are not always posted, generally, dogs are permitted on trails within the Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela. However, they should always be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock, especially when passing through rural areas and near farms.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike loop or to access the general area within the Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation and private property.

faqs.question: What notable sights or viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular natural scenery and cultural highlights. You'll pass through the historic village of Folgosinho, known for its granite houses and medieval castle, offering panoramic views. Another key viewpoint is Cabeço da Azinha, which provides stunning vistas of the surrounding hill country and valleys.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Estradão Videmonte - Folgosinho, and also intersect with trails like Rota dos Galhardos, Rota da Azinha, Rota do Corredor de Mouros, and the Grande Rota das Aldeias Históricas, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and remote sections, it's crucial to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the route downloaded is highly recommended, as signage may be limited in some areas.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the village of Folgosinho offers local gastronomy, including traditional dishes like kid goat and lamb, and is famous for Serra da Estrela cheese. There are also accommodation options available in Folgosinho and nearby villages, ranging from guesthouses to rural tourism establishments, perfect for resting after a challenging ride.
